#Cell 1 
import subprocess
import sys

# Uninstall and reinstall typing_extensions
subprocess.check_call([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "uninstall", "-y", "typing_extensions"])
subprocess.check_call([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "install", "--no-cache-dir", "typing_extensions>=4.8.0"])

print("typing_extensions upgraded. Please RESTART THE KERNEL before continuing.")
print("In Synapse: Stop the session and start a new one, or restart the notebook.")

#Cell 3

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
import os

# Enable anonymisation
os.environ["ODW_ENABLE_ANONYMISATION"] = "true"

spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()

# Test with sample data
test_data = [
    {"id": "1", "name": "John Doe", "email": "john.doe@example.com"},
    {"id": "2", "name": "Jane Smith", "email": "jane.smith@example.com"}
]
df = spark.createDataFrame(test_data)

print("Testing ServiceBusStandardisationProcess initialization:")
process = ServiceBusStandardisationProcess(spark, debug=True)
print(f"Anonymisation enabled: {process.enable_anonymisation}")
print(f"Anonymisation engine: {process.anonymisation_engine}")

# Test anonymisation directly
if process.anonymisation_engine:
    cols = [
        {"column_name": "name", "classifications": ["MICROSOFT.PERSONAL.NAME"]},
        {"column_name": "email", "classifications": ["MICROSOFT.PERSONAL.EMAIL"]}
    ]
    anonymised = process.anonymisation_engine.apply(df, cols)
    
    print("\nOriginal:")
    df.show(truncate=False)
    
    print("\nAnonymised:")
    anonymised.show(truncate=False)
